source_pr.title: executor: make TestPointGetReadLock stable
source_pr.body:
Signed-off-by: crazycs520 <crazycs520@gmail.com>

<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

```sql

[2020-12-09T05:21:58.083Z] ----------------------------------------------------------------------

[2020-12-09T05:21:58.083Z] FAIL: point_get_test.go:601: testPointGetSuite.TestPointGetReadLock

[2020-12-09T05:21:58.083Z] 

[2020-12-09T05:21:58.083Z] point_get_test.go:650:

[2020-12-09T05:21:58.083Z]     c.Assert(explain, Matches, ".*num_rpc.*")

[2020-12-09T05:21:58.083Z] ... value string = "[Point_Get_1 1.00 1 root table:point time:5.17µs, loops:2,  handle:1 N/A N/A]"

[2020-12-09T05:21:58.083Z] ... regex string = ".*num_rpc.*"
```

Same with https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/21491

### What is changed and how it works?


### Related changes

- N/A

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- No code

Side effects

- No

### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

- N/A

symptoms: TestPointGetReadLock intermittently fails because EXPLAIN ANALYZE output misses expected fields (e.g., `num_rpc`) when stale schema/lock-cache state is observed.
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky due to metadata caching / schema version timing: after lock/unlock operations, schema reload and cache release are asynchronous, so the domain/InfoSchema cache may still reflect the old state when `EXPLAIN ANALYZE` runs. This can lead to unexpected plan/operator output (e.g., Point_Get without `num_rpc`) and intermittent assertion failures. This PR stabilizes the test by adding `s.dom.Reload()` around lock/unlock so the cache state is refreshed deterministically before assertions.
fix_pattern: Force domain/InfoSchema reload (`s.dom.Reload()`) around lock/unlock before checking EXPLAIN ANALYZE output, avoiding stale schema/lock-cache visibility.

evidence: evidence: PR title: executor: make TestPointGetReadLock stable
evidence: add `s.dom.Reload()` around lock/unlock to release cache deterministically (executor/point_get_test.go)
